# UNK-167 sqlx runtime-query allowlist
#
# This file is the canonical registry of `sqlx::query(...)` /
# `sqlx::query_as(...)` / `sqlx::query_scalar(...)` invocations exempted
# from the compile-time-macro requirement. CI greps for runtime sqlx in
# `backend/src/` and rejects any line not containing a fixed string from
# this file (`grep -v -F -f`).
#
# Adding an entry: include reviewer justification in the PR description
# adding it. The carve-out class must be one of:
#   1. DDL (CREATE/DROP/ALTER TYPE) — schema mutation; macros cannot
#      validate against schema that doesn't exist yet at prepare time.
#   2. Dynamic SQL built from runtime input (rare; flag in review).
#   3. `SELECT set_config(...)` GUC mutation — Postgres session/transaction
#      configuration, not data access; macros cannot validate GUC mutation
#      against schema at prepare time.
#   4. Enum-drift test probes — `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE` + cast performed
#      at runtime in tests to detect code-vs-schema enum drift.
#
# Each non-comment line is a substring; if it appears in a `grep -rn`
# output line `path:line:content`, that runtime invocation is allowed.
#
# Two pattern styles are mixed deliberately:
#   - `path:` prefixes act as file-level blankets: every runtime sqlx
#     invocation in that file is exempted. Use only when the entire
#     file's runtime usage falls under one carve-out class. New runtime
#     additions to such files pass the guard silently — review them
#     manually against the documented carve-out classes.
#   - `<SQL substring>` patterns scope the exemption to specific call
#     shapes inside files that mix legitimate macros with carve-outs.

# (3) db.rs file-blanket — RLS context GUC plumbing (set_config + current_setting verification helper)
backend/src/db.rs:

# (1, 4) models/manifestation_format.rs file-blanket — enum-drift detection test (ALTER TYPE + ::manifestation_format probe)
backend/src/models/manifestation_format.rs:

# (1, 4) models/user.rs file-blanket — user_role enum-drift detection test (ALTER TYPE + ::user_role cast probe + role-cast fixture)
backend/src/models/user.rs:

# (3, 4) test_support.rs file-blanket — set_config('app.system_context', ...) in after_connect callback (class 3) plus role-cast drift fixtures for admin/child/adult (class 4); test-only fixture module
backend/src/test_support.rs:

# (3) services/writeback/queue.rs:~1020 RLS impostor test — exact SQL string match
# (not file-anchored because queue.rs is mostly macro-based business logic; a
# blanket would over-allow future runtime drift in the file).
SELECT set_config('app.current_user_id', $1, true)
